title: AbbVie Declares Quarterly Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: AbbVie Inc. declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.73 per share, payable August 14, 2026, to stockholders of record as of July 15, 2026. The company has increased its dividend by more than 330 percent since its inception in 2013 and is a member of the S&P Dividend Aristocrats Index.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a quarterly dividend and has demonstrated consistent dividend growth of over 330% since 2013. Membership in the S&P Dividend Aristocrats Index indicates 25+ consecutive years of annual dividend increases, reflecting strong financial health and commitment to shareholder returns.

title: Genmab Announces Epcoritamab Monotherapy and Epcoritamab-Based Combination Regimens Demonstrate High Response Rates in Elderly Patients with Newly Diagnosed Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL)
author: Genmab A/S
description: Genmab announced positive Phase 2 and Phase 1b/2 trial results for epcoritamab in elderly DLBCL patients. The monotherapy arm (EPCORE DLBCL-3) showed 67% overall response rate and 58% complete response rate, while the combination with R-mini-CHOP (EPCORE NHL-2) demonstrated 93% overall response rate and 86% complete response rate with durable remissions and sustained MRD negativity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong clinical trial results demonstrating high response rates and durable remissions for epcoritamab in elderly DLBCL patients. Both monotherapy and combination approaches showed robust efficacy with manageable safety profiles, supporting potential for expanded use and regulatory approvals in earlier lines of therapy.

As co-developer of epcoritamab with shared commercial responsibilities, positive trial results enhance the commercial potential of the drug. The data supports broader clinical utility and potential for additional regulatory approvals across multiple indications and patient populations.

title: Teva's Biosimilar Boom Is Just Getting Started. Is It Time to Buy This Rebounding Pharma Stock?
author: Alex Carchidi
description: Teva Pharmaceutical has rebounded 97% in 12 months driven by its expanding biosimilars lineup and branded drugs like Austedo. However, the stock now trades at a fair 25x P/E ratio with 2026 revenue expected to be flat to lower. While biosimilars could grow to $800 million by 2027, the narrow-margin business faces intense competition and the easy gains may already be priced in. The analyst recommends watching but not buying.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the company shows strong recovery and promising biosimilar pipeline, the analyst cautions that current valuation (25x P/E) is fair but not a bargain, 2026 revenue is expected flat to lower, and the easy gains have already been made. The recommendation is to watch but not buy.

Mentioned as the branded drug manufacturer whose Humira faces biosimilar competition from Teva's Simlandi. The article notes Humira copies have captured only about 21% of volume by late 2024, indicating the branded drug maintains significant market share despite biosimilar pressure.

Teva's biotech partner that develops and manufactures biosimilars while Teva handles U.S. commercialization. The partnership is key to Teva's biosimilar strategy, but Teva captures only a slice of proceeds due to narrow margins and high competition in the biosimilar space.

title: AbbVie Says Nine-Year Data Reinforces Venetoclax Benefit In Leukemia Patients
author: Vandana Singh
description: AbbVie announced nine-year Phase 3 CLL14 trial data showing venetoclax plus obinutuzumab significantly improved progression-free survival (6.4 years vs 3.2 years) compared to chlorambucil plus obinutuzumab in previously untreated chronic lymphocytic leukemia patients. The fixed-duration combination demonstrated median time to next treatment of 7.6 years with sustained safety and efficacy.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nine-year clinical trial data demonstrates strong efficacy of venetoclax-based therapy with significantly improved progression-free survival and durable off-treatment benefits, supporting the drug's long-term value proposition and potentially strengthening market position in leukemia treatment.

As a healthcare sector ETF with significant ABBV weighting, positive company-specific news provides modest support, but the ETF's performance is diversified across multiple holdings and the article notes the stock was only up 0.41%.
Similar to TRUH, this broad healthcare ETF benefits from ABBV's positive news but the impact is diluted across numerous healthcare holdings, with minimal stock price movement observed.

title: AbbVie Reports Promising New Clinical Updates. Here's What It Means for the Company's Dividend.
author: Jeff Siegel
description: AbbVie presented 21 clinical presentations on blood cancer therapies at the European Hematology Association Congress, showcasing progress in its oncology portfolio which generated $6.7 billion in 2025 revenue. The company's diversified pipeline across immunology, oncology, and neuroscience is helping offset Humira's patent-driven decline and could support future dividend growth through expanded cash flow.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positive clinical updates on 21 blood cancer presentations, diversified revenue streams reducing single-product dependency, and strong oncology portfolio ($6.7B revenue) supporting future dividend sustainability and growth potential.
Mentioned only as a comparison point for dividend yield (roughly 2% vs AbbVie's 3%); no specific news or analysis provided about the company.
